title: The friend I became to myself
blurb: Trading a brutal inner critic for something kinder.
lead: A story about self-hatred — and learning to speak to yourself the way you'd speak to someone you love.

blocks.text: The inner voice was relentless. Every mistake became proof of being fundamentally not good enough. It felt like honesty — like the voice was just telling the truth.
The idea of self-compassion sounded soft, even self-indulgent. Surely being hard on yourself was what kept you from failing?
The shift started with one question, learned from a book: 'Would I say this to a friend I love?' The answer was always no — the words were far too cruel. So why say them to yourself?

It felt awkward at first, offering yourself kindness. But slowly, the harsh voice got quieter, and something steadier grew in its place. Not arrogance. Just the sense of being a flawed, worthy human being — and finally being on your own side.
I spent years being my own worst critic. Learning to be my own friend changed everything.
This is an illustrative composite — a realistic story drawn from common experiences, not a real named individual. It's here to remind you that change is possible.
